name: yeeflow-app-builder
description: Yeeflow App Builder Codex plugin for building, validating, testing, and improving Yeeflow applications using Yeeflow-specific skills, including documented Yeeflow REST API capability mapping, browser OAuth API authentication, generated-final YAPK API-issued ID provenance hard gates, navigation runtime metadata hard gates, runtime-binding lessons guardrails for dashboard summaries and filters, approval real-control validation, navigation reachability checks, requester-context getUserAttr validation, app group ID and service portal payload guards, product-team YAPK schema refresh, Datetime canonical schema guardrails, app-plan conformance and navigation guardrails, product-team YAP/YAPK schema validation, Collection card-style and Collection + Grid table-style golden references, schema overlays, release safety scanning, package API automation, read-only workspace discovery and selection, and tenant-neutral environment configuration guidance.

interface.longDescription: Yeeflow App Builder Codex plugin for planning Yeeflow applications from requirements, generating and validating new .yap packages, learning from exported Yeeflow packages, inspecting and validating .yapk existing-application version packages, applying proven YAP/YAPK import-generation rules, documented Yeeflow REST API capability mapping, Browser OAuth-backed Yeeflow REST API authentication, Collection card-style and Collection + Grid table-style golden references, product-team canonical YAP/YAPK schemas with a separate Codex YAPK overlay, effective YAPK schema validation, generated-final YAP contracts, generated-final YAPK API-issued content ID provenance gates, generated-final YAPK navigation runtime metadata gates, dashboard grid-table Collection pattern gates, UI/Summary/KPI runtime hard gates, approval form designer-shape validation, workflow assignment assignee guardrails for manager-based and job-position routing, release safety scanning, ReplaceIds final coverage rules, workflow task URL page-type gates, advanced Yeeflow controls, reference-app UI templates, data-list system schema rules, read-only workspace discovery and selection, package materialization validation, app-plan-to-implementation conformance, navigation conformance guardrails, runtime-binding lessons for dashboard Summary/filter bindings, approval real-control validation, requester-context getUserAttr token wrappers, app group ID and service portal payload guards, signing/API proof boundaries, policy-as-data modeling, and official plugin release management. v0.6.45 adds application layout chrome fidelity gates for generated Yeeflow design images. Layout 1 canonical chrome requires a dark header and dark vertical navigation; rejects header hamburger icons and bottom Collapse controls; enforces multi-page consistency for applicationChromeStyleId, header/nav mode, nav background, selected state, app icon/name placement, and content safe area; treats dark/light nav drift as a hard failure; rejects unsupported SaaS shells, arbitrary sidebars, top bars, floating navs, and unsupported navigation chrome; covers the Marketing Event-inspired Event Portfolio versus Planning Workbench regression with HEADER_HAMBURGER_NOT_ALLOWED, NAV_BACKGROUND_MISMATCH, and APPLICATION_CHROME_DRIFT findings; keeps screenshot-derived rules human-reviewed or review-required; and does not claim automated screenshot parsing or pixel-perfect verification. v0.6.44 adds Yeeflow application layout design standards for generated UI design images. It treats PNG/JPEG screenshots as the primary visual source for layout geometry and YAPK exports as supporting structural references only; requires generated page images to use one consistent official Yeeflow layout across the app, covering application-layout-1-vertical-nav, application-layout-2-horizontal-nav, application-layout-3-header-nav, and application-layout-4-no-nav; adds docs/standards/yeeflow-application-layout-design-rules.md, inspect-application-layout-design-rules.mjs, and test-application-layout-design-rules.mjs; requires header/nav/content safe-area rules, page title/action placement, dropdown or expanded menu behavior, and forbidden chrome patterns; supports JSON and Markdown contracts with equivalent structured fields rather than requiring a literal visualLayoutMatrix field; rejects arbitrary SaaS shells, unsupported sidebars, unsupported top bars, floating navs, and unsupported navigation chrome; keeps screenshot-derived rules human-reviewed or review-required unless a real parser exists; and does not claim pixel-perfect verification or automated screenshot understanding. v0.6.43 adds Phase 3B UI workflow enforcement. It adds inspect-ui-closed-loop-workflow-enforcement.mjs to validate workflow/report metadata only; it does not parse or mutate real YAPK packages, call Yeeflow APIs, generate packages, sign, install, import, upgrade, or create runtime evidence. It connects Phase 1, Phase 2, and Phase 3A gates at workflow/report level; enforces required artifacts for high-quality UI, design-fidelity, runtime-quality, and dynamic-KPI claims including UI contract, UI contract validation result, scope manifest and scope validation result for upgrade work, runtime evidence and validation result, design/runtime structure comparison findings, before/after KPI mutation evidence when dynamic KPI proof is claimed, and final report artifact paths; reinforces that install/upgrade success is not visual proof, structure comparison is not dynamic KPI proof, and dynamic KPI proof requires before/after mutation evidence; documents standard generated-ui-contracts, ui-upgrade-scopes, and runtime-evidence artifact paths; and closes the planned UI-quality track so future work is normal incremental improvement unless a new runtime issue class is discovered. v0.6.42 adds Phase 3A UI closed-loop hard gates. It makes the closed-loop workflow mandatory for high-quality UI, design/mockup, dashboard redesign, runtime-proof, Marketing Event-style, and one-page-at-a-time work; requires UI contract generation and validation, a scope manifest, scope validation, local UI/package hard gates, write confirmation before signing/install/upgrade, redacted runtime evidence, design/runtime structure comparison, and iteration on exact failing controls; reinforces that package validation, signing, install, upgrade-check, and upgrade-apply success are not visual proof; reinforces that dynamic KPI proof requires before/after mutation evidence; and adds Marketing Event-inspired synthetic regressions for missing design contracts, missing runtime evidence, one-page scope drift, Summary layout-resource ReportIds/exts/tempVars, top-level Pages[].ReportIds optional-only boundaries, Summary save_var.name and COUNT ListDataID, grid-table detail-link/template/column failures, fake upgrade success evidence, missing table sections, plain scaffold/placeholder detection, warning versus --strict behavior, and dynamic KPI proof boundaries. Phase 3B adds workflow-level enforcement through inspect-ui-closed-loop-workflow-enforcement.mjs, final artifact/report path checks, and metadata gates tying together UI contract, scope, runtime evidence, design/runtime structure comparison, and dynamic KPI mutation proof boundaries. Future UI-quality work is normal incremental improvement unless a new runtime issue class appears.
interface.defaultPrompt: Use Yeeflow App Builder v0.6.45 to plan, generate, validate, and harden Yeeflow application packages. Before any Yeeflow API operation, check the REST API capability map, use only documented capabilities, check OAuth/API auth status, and if OAuth is missing or expired return plugin-login-flow guidance with auth_required / login_flow_required while preserving the original operation. Use read-only workspace and job-position discovery plus explicit target workspace confirmation for package install/import/upgrade. Require explicit confirmation for write capabilities, stronger confirmation for package install/import/upgrade/delete, and confirmed system-admin permission before job-position create/update/assign/remove writes. Treat schemas, package validators, workflow validators, assignment task assignee proof, runtime binding checks, API acceptance, plan-conformance checks, YAPK signing, generated-final YAPK ID provenance, generated-final YAPK navigation runtime metadata, YAPK upgrade ID stability, dashboard grid-table Collection validation, UI/Summary/KPI runtime evidence, aggregate UI hard-gate smoke, redacted runtime evidence templates, exact UUID Summary KPI proof, Data Analytics control identity guardrails, and approved app-plan hard gates as separate proof boundaries. Enforce Yeeflow application layout design standards and application chrome fidelity gates for generated design images, require one of the four official layouts with screenshot-primary visual rules, canonical Layout 1 dark header/dark vertical nav chrome, no header hamburger or bottom Collapse controls for Layout 1, multi-page chrome consistency, nav drift rejection, and no arbitrary app shells, then enforce standard app-plan structure, page-by-page UI implementation contracts, design/mockup-to-contract scaffolding with human review required when no reliable vision parser is available, Phase 2 design-to-runtime structural comparison without pixel-perfect visual diffing or full automatic image understanding, Phase 3A mandatory closed-loop workflow for high-quality UI/design/mockup/dashboard redesign/runtime-proof/Marketing Event-style/one-page-at-a-time work, Phase 3B workflow/report metadata enforcement with inspect-ui-closed-loop-workflow-enforcement.mjs before high-quality UI/design-fidelity claims, standard artifact path reporting, and final report evidence-chain checks, sandbox-page proof for uncertain UI/runtime patterns, export-proven Yeeflow control/style shapes, designer-shaped hidden Summary configuration with real fields, filters, temp variables, save_var expression objects, layout-resource Resource.ReportIds, matching Resource.exts, and Resource.tempVars, exact UUID Summary v1.0.1 dynamic KPI proof only when matching layout-resource ReportIds/exts/tempVars/headc variable bindings and before/after refreshed mutation evidence are present, explicit fallback labels when KPI values are not runtime-proven, UUID/runtime-safe IDs for Data Analytics controls, redacted runtime screenshot/evidence before UI-quality claims, structural design-to-runtime comparison against redacted runtime evidence before UI-quality claims, declared UI upgrade scope validation for ListSetID/app identity/page/resource/list/field/form/workflow/navigation/numeric ID drift, API-issued content ID provenance for generated-final .yapk, YAPK upgrade/new-version ID continuity for existing semantic resources, complete navigation runtime metadata, dashboard grid-table Collection wrapper/detail-link rules when planned, dashboard Summary layout-resource ReportIds/ext/tempVars bindings, filter variable consumption, approval real controls, navigation reachability, requester-context getUserAttr token wrappers, app group ID suitability, service portal gating, signing/API proof boundaries, and policy-as-data modeling before calling a generated app ready.
